name: Thermal Overload
cause: Duty cycle exceeded; internal thermal protection has activated and the machine has shut down to prevent component damage.
action: Leave Power Switch ON so the internal fan continues cooling. The welder will automatically return to service after cooling off. Reduce duration or frequency of welding periods. Refer to Duty Cycle (Duration of Use) on page 19.

name: Low Voltage Input
cause: Input voltage is below the specified limit. Can be caused by a shared circuit, insufficient amperage, or undersized wiring.
action: Inspect input voltage and ensure it is within machine limits. Verify the circuit supplies the required input voltage and amperage as detailed in the Specifications table. Do not use an extension cord — use only the supplied power cord or an identical replacement cord.

name: High Voltage Input
cause: Input voltage is higher than the specified limit.
action: Inspect input voltage and ensure it is within machine limits. The machine will resume normal operation once the issue has been remedied. There is no circuit breaker to reset.
